rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-aryl methacrylamides with IMDN-SO2CF3 to synthesize isoquinoline-1,3-diones and oxindoles via cathodically generated CF3 radicals.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of the IMDN-SO2CF3 reagent generates a CF3 radical (and releases 1-methyl-2-phenyl-1H-benzo[d]imidazole); the final cyclohexadienyl-type radical intermediate is then re-aromatized via anodic oxidation/deprotonation.

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical A (CF3-addition adduct on methacrylamide alkene)
cyclohexadienyl radical B (post-ipso cyclization onto arene)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces IMDN-SO2CF3 by single-electron transfer to release •CF3, which adds to the methacrylamide alkene to give carbon radical A; A cyclizes intramolecularly onto the aryl/amide ring to give cyclohexadienyl radical B, which is then oxidized at the anode and deprotonated to restore aromaticity and afford the isoquinoline-1,3-dione or oxindole.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; only trace 5a detected.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; only trace 5a detected.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; HRMS detected CF3–DPE adduct [M]+ 248.0812 (found 248.0807), confirming •CF3 formation from reagent 1.

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Na (Langlois reagent control)
probe_reagents.observation: No product 5a formed, indicating cathodic generation of •CF3 from Langlois reagent does not occur under these conditions.

probe_reagents.name: 1-methyl-3-phenyl-benzimidazolium BF4 (1b, lacking SO2CF3)
probe_reagents.observation: 5a obtained in 63%; demonstrates CF3 originates from the SO2CF3 portion of reagent 1 (the benzimidazolium is the redox-active carrier).

probe_reagents.name: N-methoxy methacrylamide substrate 3u
probe_reagents.observation: Desired cyclized 5u not formed; instead 5ua (34%) and 5ub (3%) observed, indicating that an anodic oxidation step is required to close the catalytic cycle/aromatize.

notes: Mechanism is supported by radical trapping (TEMPO, BHT, DPE adduct by HRMS) and by the N-OMe substrate experiment indicating an anodic re-aromatization step is operative (paired electrolysis). No KIE, CV, or DFT studies reported. Control with CF3SO2Na vs IMDN-SO2CF3 confirms the benzimidazolium-sulfonyl reagent is uniquely suited for cathodic •CF3 generation under these mild conditions.
